hypothesis: If diffusion-based generative models (CDVAE, GemsDiff, DiffCSP) are trained on curated MP/OQMD datasets, then their generated crystal structures will exhibit significantly lower DFT-relaxed RMSD, lower energy above hull (≤0.1 eV/atom), and formation energy and density distributions closer to the training set than equivalently perturbed structures, demonstrating that the chemically grounded evaluation framework can distinguish realistic from spurious generations.
